steps.content: Welcome to the pipeline editor! This quick tour will show you around so you know where to find everything: the menu bar, canvas, dockable panels, and floating windows.

The top bar holds your pipeline name and editor menus.

Each menu groups one type of command: **File** for pipeline operations, **View** for layout presets, **Runs** for submissions, **Components** for your libraries, and **Windows** for panels. A **Node** menu also appears here when you have a task selected.
Over on the right are your quick actions. Submit a run, check autosave status, jump to Settings, or open the documentation.

This is your workspace. Drag components onto it, connect tasks by linking their input and output handles, and pan or zoom around larger graphs.

Useful controls sit along the bottom: a **minimap** in the bottom-left, and **viewport controls** with **undo/redo** in the bottom-right.

The left sidebar holds your docked panels.

**Components** lets you browse and drag tasks onto the canvas. **Runs and submission** lets you submit your pipeline, and **Recent runs** shows the latest runs of this pipeline.
The right sidebar holds Pipeline Details and its properties. Set the pipeline description, tags, and notes here, and review any validation warnings.
Try clicking the **Greet** task on the canvas to select it.
Selecting a task opens its Task Properties panel here in the right sidebar. From this panel you can edit input arguments, configure node settings, define annotations, and inspect the component spec.
Windows are flexible. Try grabbing the Task Properties header and dragging it out of the dock to float it as its own window.
steps.fallbackContent: Windows are flexible. Try grabbing the Task Properties header and dragging it around the canvas.

steps.content: Nice! Now drag that floating window back onto the left or right sidebar to re-dock it.
steps.fallbackContent: Windows can be docked in either sidebar. Create the perfect layout that suits you!

steps.content: Last one. If you ever need to reconfigure your layout, the Windows menu lets you toggle panels on or off and apply a layout preset.
